name: Events & Wedding Planning
description: Enquiry to proposal to delivered event, with an Events object holding the date, venue, guest count and budget.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for an events and wedding planning agency. A contact is the person paying for and deciding on the event — a couple, a private host, or a corporate marketing or EA buyer — and there is often a second decision-maker on the same enquiry. A deal is one event from first enquiry through site visit and proposal to a signed contract and the day itself. Event dates, venues, guest counts and budgets are held on Event records; never invent a date, venue, supplier, headcount or price you were not given, and never imply availability you have not checked.

automation_rules.name: Answer an event enquiry the same day
automation_rules.description: Event enquiries go to four planners at once and the date is the scarce thing — first credible reply usually gets the call.

params.title: Reply to {{contact.first_name}} — confirm the date is open and offer two call slots
New event enquiry
params.body: Before quoting, capture: event date and whether it is fixed, guest count, venue (booked or still searching), realistic budget, and who else is deciding.

automation_rules.name: Keep a running history on every event
automation_rules.description: Writes a timeline note whenever an event deal moves stage, so a handover to the on-the-day coordinator has the full story.

params.title: Event stage updated: {{deal.title}}
params.body: Stage changed. Record what was agreed — venue, date, headcount, package, and the next deadline the client owes us (deposit, final numbers, menu choices).

automation_rules.name: Wrap up a delivered event
automation_rules.description: Fires the moment a deal reaches Contract & Deposit — the commercial win, which is when the handover has to happen.

params.title: Send {{contact.first_name}} the wrap-up and photos, and ask for a testimonial

kb_templates.company:
## About us

We are an events and wedding planning agency based in [your city], working across [regions you cover]. Replace this with your story, the number of events you deliver a year, and the kind of work you are known for.

## Practicalities

- Studio address and consultation hours
- Travel policy and any charge for events outside [radius]
- Public liability insurance and cover amount — venues will ask
- Preferred and approved supplier lists you hold
kb_templates.products:
## Our packages

- **Full planning** — from concept to the day itself, typically [%] of budget or [fee]
- **Partial planning** — you have made a start, we take it from [milestone], [fee]
- **On-the-day coordination** — [fee], booked from [n] weeks out
- **Design only** — styling, floorplan and moodboard, [fee]
- **Venue sourcing** — [fee] or commission-based

## What is not included

Be explicit: supplier costs, venue hire, overtime beyond [hours], and travel or accommodation for the team.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Qualifying an enquiry

1. Date — fixed, or flexible? A flexible date changes everything about what you can offer.
2. Venue — booked, shortlisted, or nothing yet.
3. Guest count, and whether that number is a hope or a list.
4. Budget. Ask for a range and check whether it includes venue and catering; most enquirers quote a figure that does not.
5. Who else decides — partner, parents paying, or a marketing director signing off.

## Proposal follow-up

Call three working days after sending. Do not ask whether they read it — ask which of the two directions they preferred, and hold the date for [n] days in writing.
kb_templates.process:
## From enquiry to the day itself

1. **New Enquiry** — log the contact, date, venue status and rough headcount.
2. **Discovery Call** — 30 minutes on vision, budget reality and decision-makers.
3. **Venue Site Visit** — walk the space, note load-in, power, wet-weather plan and curfew.
4. **Proposal Sent** — two costed directions, timeline and payment schedule.
5. **Contract & Deposit** — signed contract and deposit received; the date is now held.
6. **In Production** — supplier bookings, run sheet, final numbers and rehearsal.
7. **Event Delivered** — event run, final invoice settled; ask for photos and a review while it is fresh.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Why clients choose us

Replace with your genuine differentiators. What usually decides it: venues you have actually worked before, suppliers who answer your calls, a run sheet that survives contact with a real wedding day, and one named person on site.

## Common comparisons

- **Venue in-house coordinator** — included in the venue fee, but works for the venue, not for the client, and stops at the venue's edges.
- **A friend or family member coordinating** — free, and reliably the reason something goes wrong at 4pm.
- **Larger agencies** — deep resources, but the person who pitched is rarely the person on site.
